mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% while CPI inflation accelerates above its 12-month trend, the real policy rate turns negative, signaling monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.08% and CPIAUCSL at 276.724 suggest accommodative real rates
regime_hint: monetary easing with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger is active, or forward returns in triggered periods do not exceed non-triggered baseline by at least 2% annualized (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0% signals tight labor market conditions that compress wage growth expectations and reduce corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn negative.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.6% indicates tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window during tight labor market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS deflated by CPIAUCSL) decline month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.4 million annualized units, signaling demand destruction that precedes negative earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 1-month change < -1.0% AND HOUST < 1400 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 625416M and HOUST at 1555K suggest moderate demand
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window when both triggers are active, or negative returns occur in < 50% of triggered 20-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above its 6-month trend while remaining below 102.0 index points signals supply-side expansion constrained by capacity limits, reducing profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as inflation expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 100.0178 indicates modest production levels
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or forward returns in triggered periods do not show statistically significant negative bias (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month while unemployment rate remains stable, signaling labor market softening that precedes earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth < 100K AND UNRATE change < 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 148319K and UNRATE at 4.6% show strong labor market
regime_hint: labor market deceleration without unemployment spike
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) remain above 1.5 million units, signaling robust demand that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 19465.195B and HOUST at 1555K indicate solid demand backdrop
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in triggered periods do not exceed non-triggered baseline by at least 1.5% annualized (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 5.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, signaling real rate compression that triggers equity risk premium expansion and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as inflation expectations become unanchored.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 276.724 and FEDFUNDS at 0.08% show potential inflation-rate mismatch
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.04 over any 1-year rolling window when trigger is active, or negative returns occur in < 52% of triggered 5-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while unemployment rate rises above 5.0%, signaling simultaneous supply and labor market weakness that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as recession risk increases.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 100.0178 and UNRATE at 4.6% show no current weakness signal
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window when both triggers are active, or forward returns in triggered periods do not show statistically significant negative bias (t-test p > 0.12).
